author: Vassilios Kaxiras
name: In DRAM compute quantized ternary + int4
blurb: Trained on 2xH100 SXM for 40 minutes. Ternary-quantized 10x1024 architecture targeting in-DRAM inference on unmodified DDR4 (RowCopy + MAJ3 primitives, per Kubo et al. 2025). Q/K/V/MLP weights are quantized to ternary + offset in 128-element blocks via a custom BitLinear; K and V activations are quantized to int4 with per-block offsets and scalings (SageAttention-style on K). STE makes training quantization-aware. Because ternary weights pack densely, the model widens to dim=1024 and deepens to 10 layers while keeping FFN and attention entirely in 4-bit arithmetic — matching or beating GPU latency and cutting energy ~75% per the in-DRAM GeMV reference.
